stellen.beschreibung: Geförderter Freiwilligendienst im Europäischen Solidaritätskorps (ESC) in Hoima, Uganda. Volunteer in Hoima, Western Uganda, as part of a friendly team of 4 EU volunteers supporting conservation, schools and community action. You will work with VC Uganda, Justice and Peace Commission Hoima and the Bulindi Chimpanzee and Community Project, whose work with wild chimpanzees featured in BBC’s Planet Earth III with Sir David Attenborough. This is hands-on: planting trees, helping in tree nurseries, caring for seedlings, supporting reforestation, visiting schools and community groups, and helping deliver simple workshops on conservation, climate action and care for the environment. Geförderter Freiwilligendienst im Europäischen Solidaritätskorps (ESC) in Budapest, Ungarn. Cargonomia is looking for two motivated volunteers to join our community-based sustainability project in Budapest for a 6-month European Solidarity Corps volunteering experience. The project connects urban and rural initiatives between Budapest and the village of Zsámbok, where we also develop agroecology, eco-construction, and food processing activities. This volunteering experience combines practical involvement, community life, non-formal learning, and participation in a dynamic international network working on solidarity and sustainability. Geförderter Freiwilligendienst im Europäischen Solidaritätskorps (ESC) in Siwa Oasis, Ägypten. L'association d'accueil des volontaires CES est Torraha. Son nom évoque à la fois le filet de pêche et la tenue traditionnelle des femmes d’Alexandrie, deux piliers culturels majeurs. Torraha se consacre à la création, à l'organisation et à la gestion de programmes culturels innovants. En utilisant l'art comme levier de développement, elle offre des opportunités uniques aux personnes défavorisées. Danse, musique, littérature – autant de domaines explorés pour construire un avenir équilibré et dynamique. Tel un filet tissé avec soin, Torraha relie les éléments du passé au présent, unissant …

stellen.beschreibung: Geförderter Freiwilligendienst im Europäischen Solidaritätskorps (ESC) in St. Pölten, Österreich. Train as a place-based educator with Green Steps. Green Steps is a small international NGO which was originally founded by two Europeans in Shanghai. We support schools to practice place-based learning by extending classrooms into nature. We design pedagogical trails in city parks and suburban spaces to support with our activities the development of environmental empathy, systems understanding and bioregional stewardship.
